mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 372034M as of 2007-04-01 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative SPX returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 3+ months, monetary tightening constrains equity valuations by raising the risk-free discount rate and reducing net present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2007-04-01 indicates restrictive monetary stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 3+ months, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns is positive and > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, triggering negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations contract. The mechanism activates when month-over-month payroll growth turns negative.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative PAYEMS momentum, or Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal real economic capacity utilization. When both decline simultaneously, the mechanism indicates demand destruction and profit margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures supply-side stress that single-series signals miss.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.49 and PAYEMS at 137684K as of 2007-04-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on INDPRO and PAYEMS changes, or the interaction explains less than 2% additional variance beyond additive effects.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture broad-based demand momentum. When both accelerate, forward earnings expectations rise and equity valuations expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction reflects synchronized consumer and construction sector strength.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1518K and RSAFS at 372034M as of 2007-04-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint acceleration of HOUST and RSAFS produces positive 60-day returns in < 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.0% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase the federal funds rate, constraining equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth. The mechanism activates when inflation crosses the 3.0% threshold.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when CPI 12-month change > 3.0%, or Rank IC between CPI inflation rate and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations decline. The mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE level and 5-day forward returns is positive or near zero (< 0.01 in absolute value) over any 2-year rolling window, or rising UNRATE periods produce negative SPX returns in < 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture aggregate demand strength. When both decelerate simultaneously (growth rates fall below their 4-quarter moving averages), the mechanism signals demand-side weakness and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures synchronized economic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 4-quarter MA AND RSAFS 3-month change < 3-month MA
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11549.1B and RSAFS at 372034M as of 2007-04-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on GDPC1 and RSAFS changes, or synchronized deceleration produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of instances.
